title: Volume estimates for unions of convex sets, and the Kakeya set conjecture

thm:1.1.summary: Every Kakeya set in $\mathbb{R}^3$ has Minkowski and Hausdorff dimension $3$ — the Kakeya set conjecture in three dimensions, resolved.

thm:1.2.summary: Quantitative form: for a set $\mathbb{T}$ of $\delta$-tubes in the unit ball of $\mathbb{R}^3$ obeying the Katz–Tao convex Wolff axioms, the union has near-maximal volume $\mu(\mathbb{T}) \lessapprox 1$ — the maximal-function statement from which dimension $3$ follows.

def:1.3.summary: Katz–Tao Convex Wolff Axioms: $C_{KT\text{-}CW}(\mathbb{T})$ bounds $\#\{T \in \mathbb{T}: T \subset W\} \leq C|W||T|^{-1}$ over all convex $W$ — the non-concentration hypothesis under which the volume estimate is proved.

def:1.5.summary: Assertions $\mathcal{D}(\sigma,\omega)$ and $\mathcal{E}(\sigma,\omega)$: a two-parameter family of scale-invariant volume/density estimates for $\delta^\eta$-dense tube families obeying the Wolff axioms — the induction variables of the whole proof.

prop:1.6.summary: Equivalence: for $0 \leq \sigma \leq 2/3$, $\mathcal{E}(\sigma,\omega) \Longleftrightarrow \mathcal{D}(\sigma,\omega)$ — the two assertion families are interchangeable, letting the induction move between them.

prop:1.7.summary: Strict gain (the engine): for $0 \leq \sigma \leq 2/3$, $\omega > 0$, $\mathcal{E}(\sigma,\omega) \implies \mathcal{D}(\sigma, \omega - g(\sigma,\omega))$ with $g > 0$ — each induction step strictly improves the defect $\omega$.

prop:1.8.summary: Base case: $\mathcal{D}(1/2,0)$ is true — the anchoring estimate the multiscale induction starts from.

thm:1.9.summary: Endpoint: $\mathcal{D}(0,0)$ and $\mathcal{E}(0,0)$ are true — the limit of the induction, the assertion that directly yields the Kakeya estimate.

cor:1.10.summary: Volume estimate: for a $\lambda$-dense family, $|\bigcup_T Y(T)| \geq \delta^\varepsilon \lambda^K m^{-1}(\#\mathbb{T})|T|$ with $m = C_{KT\text{-}CW}(\mathbb{T})$ — the concrete consequence of the endpoint assertion feeding the main theorem.

conjecture:1.11.summary: Strengthened convex-Wolff conjecture in $\mathbb{R}^n$: $|\bigcup_T \tilde{T}| \leq \delta^{-\varepsilon}|\bigcup_T T|$ (tube-doubling) — the natural higher-dimensional generalization the $\mathbb{R}^3$ result motivates.

edges.note: Dimension $3$ follows from the quantitative near-maximal volume estimate by a standard maximal-function argument.
The maximal estimate is the convex-Wolff volume corollary specialized to the Kakeya setting.
The volume estimate is the concrete content of the endpoint assertions $\mathcal{D}(0,0)/\mathcal{E}(0,0)$.
Reaching $\omega = 0$ uses the strict-gain step iterated: each application lowers the defect by $g > 0$.
The induction alternates between $\mathcal{D}$ and $\mathcal{E}$ via their equivalence.
The induction is anchored at the base case $\mathcal{D}(1/2,0)$.
Stated in terms of the assertion families $\mathcal{D}(\sigma,\omega)$, $\mathcal{E}(\sigma,\omega)$.
The strict-gain step is a relation between the assertion families.
The base case is an instance of assertion $\mathcal{D}$.
The assertions are quantified over families obeying the Katz–Tao convex Wolff axioms.
The $\mathbb{R}^3$ convex-Wolff estimate motivates the general-$n$ tube-doubling conjecture.

main_results.description: The Kakeya set conjecture in $\mathbb{R}^3$ and its quantitative maximal form.

framework.description: The Katz–Tao convex Wolff axioms and the two-parameter assertion families that are the induction variables.

multiscale_induction.description: Base case, equivalence, and strict-gain step, iterated to the endpoint assertions.

consequences.description: The volume estimate feeding the main theorem, and the generalization conjectured.

Gut.title: Guth, The Kakeya conjecture, after Wang and Zahl
Gut.role: the survey of this paper (companion node in this DAG)

KT02.title: Katz–Tao, New bounds for Kakeya problems

Wol95.role: the Wolff axioms and hairbrush method, foundational to the framework

Bou91.role: the bush argument / discretized sum-product lineage

WZ22.role: the sticky-case precursor this paper completes
